events.label: Grand Canyon mid-air
events.description: Disaster accelerates federal radar ATC modernization in United States.

name: Air Traffic Controller
summary: A specialist who sequences aircraft takeoffs, landings, and en-route separation using radar and radio—turning crowded skies into orderly flow from tower cabs to oceanic centers.
duties: Issue clearances, monitor separation minima, coordinate with adjacent sectors, manage weather diversions, log incidents.
skills: Spatial reasoning, stress tolerance, phraseology precision, rapid prioritization.
tools: Radar scopes, STARS/ERAM systems, headsets, flight strips digitally or paper.
regions: FAA, Eurocontrol, and national ANSP facilities at major airports worldwide.
eventDescription: Disaster accelerates federal radar ATC modernization in United States.
educationPath: FAA Academy or national ATC school, ongoing simulator checks.
modernSectors: Tower, TRACON, en-route centers, military control, private contract towers.

events.label: Jet age maintenance
events.description: Turbine engines and pressurized hulls raise specialization and tooling standards.

name: Aircraft Mechanic
summary: A technician who inspects, repairs, and certifies airframes and engines to keep aircraft airworthy—licensed A&P or equivalent under strict regulatory traceability.
duties: Perform scheduled inspections, troubleshoot systems, replace components, sign logbooks, follow airworthiness directives.
skills: Hydraulics, avionics basics, sheet metal, regulatory literacy, safety discipline.
tools: Torque wrenches, borescope, maintenance manuals, work order systems.
regions: Airline MRO hangars, general aviation shops, military depots globally.
eventDescription: Turbine engines and pressurized hulls raise specialization and tooling standards.
educationPath: FAA Part 147 schools, apprenticeships, IA authorization for inspectors.
modernSectors: Airlines, cargo, MRO contractors, business aviation, defense contractors.

events.label: Integrated briefing
events.description: Automated flight service and centralized weather units streamline pilot briefings.

name: Aviation Meteorologist
summary: A forecaster focused on ceiling, visibility, icing, and winds for flight safety—briefing dispatchers and controllers with TAFs and SIGMETs tailored to aviation.
duties: Issue terminal forecasts, monitor convective weather, staff CWSU desks at centers, advise on diversions.
skills: Synoptic analysis, radar interpretation, ICAO phraseology, time-critical communication.
tools: AWIPS, TAF editors, lightning mappers, METAR/TAF databases.
regions: National weather services co-located with ATC; airline meteorology hubs.
eventDescription: Automated flight service and centralized weather units streamline pilot briefings.
educationPath: Meteorology degree, aviation weather certification modules.
modernSectors: FAA CWSU, airlines, private briefing providers, military weather flights.

events.label: Deregulation hiring waves
events.description: U.S. airline competition expands hiring and changes service models.

name: Flight Attendant
summary: A cabin crew member who ensures passenger safety and service aloft—from preflight briefings to emergency evacuations and hospitality on long-haul jets.
duties: Demonstrate safety equipment, serve meals, manage conflicts, assist medical events, secure cabin for landing.
skills: CRM teamwork, first aid, conflict de-escalation, multilingual service, grooming standards.
tools: Safety demo kit, galley equipment, PA system, restraint policies.
regions: Global airlines; profession feminized and unionized differently by country.
originLabel: Airline steward roles on passenger liners
eventDescription: U.S. airline competition expands hiring and changes service models.
educationPath: Airline training academies, safety drills, CPR certification.
modernSectors: Passenger airlines, business aviation, charter, VIP government jets.

events.label: Post-9/11 security overlays
events.description: Dispatch integrates new security protocols and airspace closures into planning.

name: Flight Dispatcher
summary: An airline operations controller who plans fuel, routes, and legality of flights jointly responsible with the captain—OC desks humming with weather and weight-balance math.
duties: File flight plans, compute fuel, monitor en-route progress, authorize releases, re-route around storms.
skills: Meteorology, aircraft performance, regulations, software fluency, shift stamina.
tools: Dispatch software, ACARS messaging, weather charts, NOTAM feeds.
regions: Airline operations centers in Dallas, Frankfurt, Dubai, and hub cities.
eventDescription: Dispatch integrates new security protocols and airspace closures into planning.
educationPath: Dispatcher certificate courses, airline type-specific training.
modernSectors: Network airlines, cargo carriers, fractional jet operators.

events.label: Ground outsourcing
events.description: Airlines contract ramp work to specialized handlers at many stations.

name: Ramp Agent
summary: A ground handler who marshals aircraft, loads bags, and connects ground power on the apron—high-visibility vest labor keeping turnarounds on schedule.
duties: Guide aircraft parking, operate belt loaders, drive tugs, report FOD hazards, secure doors.
skills: Team radio calls, safety around jet blast, physical endurance, winter deicing coordination.
tools: Headset, marshalling wands, belt loader, baggage tug, chocks.
regions: Every commercial airport; often outsourced to ground service companies.

eventDescription: Airlines contract ramp work to specialized handlers at many stations.
educationPath: Airport badging, GSE training, OSHA safety modules.
modernSectors: Ground handlers, airlines, freight integrators, regional airports.

events.label: Hydrant fuel systems
events.description: Underwing hydrants reduce truck traffic at large hubs.

name: Aircraft Fueler
summary: A ramp worker who refuels aircraft from hydrant carts or tankers—calculating uplift against temperature and weight limits with fire-safe procedures.
duties: Connect hoses, monitor gallons, complete fuel tickets, conduct bonding, report spills.
skills: Hazmat awareness, math for density, night ramp safety, communication with cockpit.
tools: Fuel truck or hydrant cart, meters, bonding cables, PPE.
regions: Commercial airports; military POL units parallel role.
eventDescription: Underwing hydrants reduce truck traffic at large hubs.
educationPath: Fuel farm training, fire safety, airport security badge.
modernSectors: Fuel consortiums, FBOs, airline contracted fueling.

events.label: Glass cockpit era
events.description: Integrated displays shift maintenance toward software and databus diagnostics.

name: Avionics Technician
summary: A specialist who installs and repairs aircraft electronic navigation, communication, and flight-control systems—glass cockpits demanding software as much as soldering.
duties: Test transponders, repair wiring harnesses, update FMS databases, calibrate autopilot, document STC mods.
skills: Electronics, schematic reading, regulatory paperwork, troubleshooting intermittent faults.
tools: Multimeter, avionics bench, pitot-static test set, laptop loaders.

events.label: Colgan rule impacts
events.description: Airline hiring minimums reshape CFI as time-building career stage.

name: Flight Instructor
summary: A certificated pilot who teaches others to fly—dual controls in small trainers through instrument and multi-engine ratings, building hours toward airline careers.
duties: Demonstrate maneuvers, sign logbook endorsements, brief weather, enforce safety, prepare students for checkrides.
skills: Pedagogy, patience, stick-and-rudder mastery, risk management narration.
tools: Training aircraft, simulator, syllabus, FAA practical test standards.

events.label: Modern sport ballooning
events.description: FAI records and tourist economies sustain professional balloonist niche.

name: Balloonist
summary: An aeronaut who flies lighter-than-air craft by heating air or using gas lift—Montgolfier spectacles to modern sport and tourist rides at dawn.
duties: Inflate envelope, read winds, navigate visually, land in open fields, pack and trailer equipment.
skills: Weather judgment, burner management, passenger briefing, chase crew coordination.
tools: Burner, propane tanks, wicker basket, anemometer, chase truck radio.
regions: Albuquerque, Cappadocia, Loire Valley, festival circuits.

events.label: Hindenburg disaster
events.description: Passenger airship travel collapses; riggers fade with lighter-than-air decline.

name: Airship Rigger
summary: A crew member who maintained dirigible envelopes, ballast, and mooring lines in the golden age of zeppelins—skilled height work largely ended by hydrogen disasters and WWII.
duties: Patch gas cells, manage ballast, moor to masts, inspect ribs and engines on rigid ships.
skills: Rope work at height, gas handling, puncture repair, meteorology for long voyages.
tools: Mooring mast, gas valves, rigging knives, altimeters.
regions: Germany, Britain, and U.S. naval air stations, 1900s–1930s peak.
eventDescription: Passenger airship travel collapses; riggers fade with lighter-than-air decline.

events.label: Automation arrives
events.description: Computer tracking supplants manual plotting in ATC and air defense.

name: Radar Operator
summary: A military or civil technician who interpreted raw radar returns before automation—tracking aircraft and weather echoes from Cold War bunkers to early ATC cabs.
duties: Scope interpretation, plot tracks, report contacts, maintain set calibration, coordinate intercepts militarily.
skills: Pattern recognition, radio procedure, electronics maintenance basics.
tools: Plan position indicator scopes, IFF interrogators, log boards.
regions: WWII and Cold War air defense chains; early civilian en-route centers.

eventDescription: Computer tracking supplants manual plotting in ATC and air defense.

events.label: ARFF standards harmonized
events.description: FAA and ICAO rules tighten response times and foam quantities.

name: Airport Firefighter
summary: A rescue specialist trained for aircraft fuel fires and mass evacuation on runways—ARFF trucks with foam cannons staged under response-time regulations.
duties: Drill monthly, respond to alerts, extricate occupants, apply agent on fuel fires, coordinate with ATC.
skills: Hazmat foam tactics, high-speed truck operation, EMS, live-fire drills.
tools: ARFF vehicle, proximity suits, hydraulic rescue tools, foam concentrate.

eventDescription: FAA and ICAO rules tighten response times and foam quantities.

events.label: Deregulation safety oversight
events.description: Inspector general and airline QA roles expand amid competitive maintenance outsourcing.

name: Aircraft Inspector
summary: A regulator or airline inspector who audits maintenance and airworthiness—FAA IA or national equivalent signing off annuals and overseeing compliance cultures.
duties: Audit work cards, witness tests, investigate incidents, approve return to service, enforce ADs.
skills: Regulatory mastery, forensic teardown judgment, interview technique, documentation rigor.
tools: Maintenance records, borescope, checklists, enforcement manuals.

events.label: Bag fee restructuring

name: Baggage Handler
summary: An airport worker who sorts and loads luggage into aircraft holds or carousels—physically demanding role central to mishandled-bag statistics and hub pressure.
duties: Scan tags, build carts, load bins weight-balanced, deliver to carousels, report damaged items.

summary: A maintainer specializing in rotorcraft dynamic components—tracking blade life, swashplate lubrication, and vibration signatures unlike fixed-wing shops.

summary: A control-room technician who monitors boilers, turbines, and generators delivering grid electricity—balancing load, emissions, and safety interlocks 24/7.
duties: Start/stop units, adjust megawatts, log parameters, respond to trips, coordinate with dispatch.

summary: A utility climber who builds and repairs high-voltage transmission and distribution lines—storm chasers restoring power from bucket trucks and hot sticks.
duties: Set poles, string conductor, splice cable, switch circuits, respond to outages.

summary: A panel operator who runs distillation, cracking, and treating units converting crude into fuels—chemical plant discipline with flame and H2S ever present.

summary: A control-room worker specialized in pulverized coal boilers and ash handling—retiring cohort as plants close under climate policy though some retrofits linger.
duties: Manage mills, sootblowers, SCR units, ash ponds compliance, coordinate fuel piles.
skills: Coal combustion quirks, emissions compliance, legacy DCS, decommission planning.
tools: Coal pile dozers, baghouse monitors, limestone slurry systems.
regions: U.S. Midwest, Poland, India, China coal belts.
eventDescription: Cheap gas and renewables hasten coal operator workforce decline in West.
educationPath: Power plant operator training with coal-specific units.
modernSectors: Legacy baseload plants, biomass conversions, retirement contractors.

events.label: RTO formation
events.description: Independent system operators professionalize grid operator as market role.

name: Grid Operator
summary: A balancing authority dispatcher who matches generation to load second-by-second—ISO control rooms preventing cascading blackouts with redispatch and reserves.
duties: Monitor ACE, commit reserves, clear markets operationally, shed load if needed, coordinate outages.
skills: Power flow intuition, market rules, calm during contingencies, communication with plant operators.
tools: EMS screens, interchange schedules, satellite phones for black start drills.
regions: PJM, ERCOT, National Grid control centers; national dispatch worldwide.
eventDescription: Independent system operators professionalize grid operator as market role.
educationPath: NERC certification, system operator training programs, shift mentorship.
modernSectors: ISO/RTO control rooms, utility control centers, merchant trading floors adjacent.
